paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jacob called unto his sons, and said: gather yourselves together, that I may tell you that which shall befall you in the latter days.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Assemble yourselves, and hear, ye sons of Jacob; And hearken unto Israel your father.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Reuben, thou art my first-born, my might, and the       beginning of my strength; The pre-eminence of dignity, and the pre-eminence of power.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Boiling over as water, thou shalt not have the       pre-eminence; Because thou wentest up to thy father`s bed; Then defiledst thou it: he went up to my couch.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Simeon and Levi are brethren; Weapons of violence are their swords.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: O my soul, come not thou into their council; Unto their assembly, my glory, be not thou united; For in their anger they slew a man, And in their self-will they hocked an ox.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Cursed be their anger, for it was fierce; And their wrath, for it was cruel: I will divide them in Jacob, And scatter them in Israel.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Judah, thee shall thy brethren praise: Thy hand shall be on the neck of thine enemies; Thy father`s sons shall bow down before thee.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Judah is a lion`s whelp; From the prey, my son, thou art gone up: He stooped down, he couched as a lion, And as a lioness; who shall rouse him up?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The sceptre shall not depart from Judah, Nor the ruler`s staff from between his feet, Until Shiloh come: And unto him shall the obedience of the peoples be.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Binding his foal unto the vine, And his ass`s colt unto the choice vine; He hath washed his garments in wine, And his vesture in the blood of grapes:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: His eyes shall be red with wine, And his teeth white with milk.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Zebulun shall dwell at the haven of the sea; And he shall be for a haven of ships; And his border shall be upon Sidon.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Issachar is a strong ass, Couching down between the sheepfolds:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he saw a resting-place that it was good, And the land that it was pleasant; And he bowed his shoulder to bear, And became a servant under taskwork.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Dan shall judge his people, As one of the tribes of Israel.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Dan shall be a serpent in the way, An adder in the path, That biteth the horse`s heels, So that his rider falleth backward.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: I have waited for thy salvation, O Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Gad, a troop shall press upon him; But he shall press upon their heel.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Out of the Asher his bread shall be fat, And he shall yield royal dainties.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Naphtali is a hind let loose: He giveth goodly words.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Joseph is a fruitful bough, A fruitful bough by a fountain; His branches run over the wall.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The archers have sorely grieved him, And shot at him, and persecute him:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But his bow abode in strength, And the arms of his hands were made strong, By the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob, (From thence is the shepherd, the stone of Israel),

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Even by the God of thy father, who shall help thee, And by the Almighty, who shall bless thee, With blessings of heaven above, Blessings of the deep that coucheth beneath, Blessings of the breasts, and of the womb.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The blessings of thy father Have prevailed above the blessings of my progenitors Unto the utmost bound of the everlasting hills: They shall be on the head of Joseph, And on the crown of the head of him that was separate from his       brethren.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Benjamin is a wolf that raveneth: In the morning she shall devour the prey, And at even he shall divide the spoil.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: All these are the twelve tribes of Israel: and this is it that their father spake unto them and blessed them; every one according to his blessing he blessed them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he charged them, and said unto them, I am to be gathered unto my people: bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: in the cave that is in the field of Machpelah, which is before Mamre, in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought with the field from Ephron the Hittite for a possession of a burying-place.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: there they buried Abraham and Sarah his wife; there they buried Isaac and Rebekah his wife; and there I buried Leah:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: the field and the cave that is therein, which was purchased from the children of Heth.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And when Jacob made an end of charging his sons, he gathered up his feet into the bed, and yielded up the ghost, and was gathered unto his people.
